title: 工具账本：Microsoft 模型能力变化
summary: Microsoft announced two new text LLMs this morning - MAI-Thinking-1 (reasoning, 1T parameters, 35B active, available to "select early partners") and MAI-Code-1-Flash (137B Parameters, 5B active, "purpose-built for GitHub Copilot and VS Code to deliver high performance and lower cost [...] rolling out to GitHub Copilot individual users in Visual Studio Code"). I've not been able to try either of them just yet. It's very interesting to see Microsoft releasing models with such low parameter counts, especially given how expensive larger models are to access right now. They claim MAI-Thinking-1 "is preferred to Sonnet 4.6 in our blind human side-by-side evaluations", which is impressive for a 35B model seeing as I frequently run models larger than that on my own laptop. (UPDATE: I got this entirely wrong, see note below.) Also of note : We trained [MAI-Thinking-1] from the ground up on enterp
source_category_title: AI 工具与开发者平台
topic_direction_description: AI 模型、Agent、开发工具、API、云服务和底层能力变化。重点看这次变化到底是什么、时间线如何、会不会改变普通技术人的工作方式、成本结构和工具链。
reader_hook: 这条线索可能影响 AI/API/云服务/开发工具成本，适合按普通技术人的账本拆。
why_now: 来自本批次稳定公开源，适合先进入 Radar 观察。
reason: 命中老花主线关键词，且来源可复查。
collection_fit: 部分符合收集原则：可以进入简报观察，但证据链或读者入口还不够完整。
investigation_direction: 优先追价格页、额度、API 文档、替代方案和实际成本边界。
uncertainty_flags: 尚未抓取正文外的补充证据。
尚未形成多源交叉验证。
当前仅适合简报观察，不宜写成深度结论。
